title: pseudolysimachion longifolium subsp. septentrionale
intro: Pseudolysimachion longifolium subsp. septentrionale, also known as northern bog myrtle or northern bog-whortle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. It is a perennial herb that grows in wetlands and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and inflammation, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Pseudolysimachion longifolium subsp. septentrionale, such as infusions and tinctures, are used to relieve coughs and soothe irritated tissues.
